channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: Mysterious 'little red dots' at the beginning time may finally have an explanation, thanks to newfound 'little blue companions' - Live Science
item.description:
1. Mysterious 'little red dots' at the beginning time may finally have an explanation, thanks to newfound 'little blue companions'  Live Science
  2. One idea, two cosmic mysteries—linking Little Red Dots and globular clusters  Phys.org
  3. Little red dot galaxies may solve a cosmic neutrino mystery  Yahoo
  4. The James Webb Space Telescope found tiny “little red dots” that looked like impossibly compact galaxies — but astronomers now suspect many may be “black hole stars,” young black holes wrapped in gas so dense that the material falling into them produces  Space Daily
  5. A mysterious “red dot” spotted in space: are we witnessing a cosmic birth?  Futura, le média qui explore le monde

item.title: Did Viking 1 find life on Mars 50 years ago? Here's why the debate continues - Space
item.description:
1. Did Viking 1 find life on Mars 50 years ago? Here's why the debate continues  Space
  2. Viking: 50 Years on Mars — Poster, Wallpapers, and Backgrounds  NASA Science (.gov)
  3. Photos: 50 Years Since Viking 1 Landed on Mars  The Atlantic
  4. On This Day: Viking 1 is first spacecraft to land on Mars  upi.com
  5. Monday briefing: 50 years after Viking landed on Mars, did Nasa overlook evidence of life?  The Guardian

item.title: NASA has just released a jaw-dropping video of a spacecraft's view as it zoomed past Mars. You've got to see this - BBC Sky at Night Magazine
item.description:
1. NASA has just released a jaw-dropping video of a spacecraft's view as it zoomed past Mars. You've got to see this  BBC Sky at Night Magazine

  4. Oxygen "disappearing" from earth's waters at alarming rate, scientists warn  Oceanographic Magazine

item.title: An asteroid shattered near Jupiter’s gravitational gateway 800 million years ago; scientists say the debr - The Times of India
item.description:
1. An asteroid shattered near Jupiter’s gravitational gateway 800 million years ago; scientists say the debr  The Times of India
  2. Ancient Asteroid Barrage Traced to Jupiter Gravity Gate That Still Threatens Earth Today  Tech Times
  3. Asteroid breakup 800 million years ago may have triggered a bombardment of the inner Solar System  Universe Space Tech
  4. One cause for “Snowball Earth”? Asteroid collision with dramatic consequences  heise online
  5. A shattered asteroid may have bombarded Earth 800 million years ago  ScienceDaily

item.title: In 1977, NASA launched two probes designed to last five years. Voyager 1 and 2 are now the only human-made objects in interstellar space, still operating, still transmitting, funded by a team of engineers some of whom have worked on the mission for nearly 5 - Space Daily
item.description:
1. In 1977, NASA launched two probes designed to last five years. Voyager 1 and 2 are now the only human-made objects in interstellar space, still operating, still transmitting, funded by a team of engineers some of whom have worked on the mission for nearly 5  Space Daily
  2. Voyager 1 on track to reach one light-day from Earth  it-online.co.za
  3. NASA's Voyager 1 Radio Transmitter Is Weaker Than a Refrigerator Lightbulb. It Still Reaches Earth From 15 Billion Miles Away  19FortyFive
  4. The engineers who built Voyager knew they would almost certainly be dead before it left the solar system, and they built it anyway, a gift from people who would never see where their work would go  ScienceBlog.com
  5. Voyager 1 has no engine running. The 38,000-mile-per-hour speed it has held since its Saturn flyby in 1980 is borrowed momentum from planets it grazed more than four decades ago, and that free ride carries it to one light-day from Earth this November  Space Daily

item.title: A radio structure thought for 40 years to tower over the Milky Way’s centre is actually a nearby stellar bubble — not the fossil scar of a black hole eruption - Space Daily
item.description:
1. A radio structure thought for 40 years to tower over the Milky Way’s centre is actually a nearby stellar bubble — not the fossil scar of a black hole eruption  Space Daily
  2. A Giant Structure Is Towering Over The Milky Way. After 40 Years, Scientists Have Worked Out What It Is  ScienceAlert
  3. Scientists solve 40-year mystery of the "lobe" above the center of the Milky Way  NEWS.am TECH

item.title: In 1970, Venera 7 appeared to fall silent when it hit Venus, but engineers later found 23 minutes of faint radio data buried in the recording — the first transmission ever received from the surface of another planet - Space Daily
item.description: In 1970, Venera 7 appeared to fall silent when it hit Venus, but engineers later found 23 minutes of faint radio data buried in the recording — the first transmission ever received from the surface of another planet  Space Daily

item.title: Cassini watched bright “islands” appear, disappear and reappear in Titan’s methane seas — and laboratory experiments suggest they may be fields of nitrogen bubbles fizzing out of solution as the alien lakes warm or mix - Space Daily
item.description:
1. Cassini watched bright “islands” appear, disappear and reappear in Titan’s methane seas — and laboratory experiments suggest they may be fields of nitrogen bubbles fizzing out of solution as the alien lakes warm or mix  Space Daily
  2. Saturn's moon Titan runs the same weather cycle as Earth — clouds, rain, rivers and seas — except the liquid doing all the work is methane, and the bedrock underfoot is water frozen at nearly minus 180 degrees, harder than most stone on Earth  Space Daily
  3. Saturn’s Largest Moon May Hold the Resources for a Space Colony  SciTechDaily
  4. Even at noon Titan sits in permanent dusk: distance and thick haze together leave barely a thousandth of Earth's sunlight at the surface — about as dim as Earth ten minutes after sunset, a golden gloom that never lifts  Space Daily
  5. Titan is the only place beyond Earth where a spacecraft has landed on a surface with liquid nearby, and the probe sent back the sound of alien wind before falling silent forever  Space Daily

item.title: Across Titan’s equator, winds have built dunes around 100 metres high and hundreds of kilometres long — but the grains are water ice coated in hydrocarbons that fell out of the moon’s orange atmosphere - Space Daily
item.description: Across Titan’s equator, winds have built dunes around 100 metres high and hundreds of kilometres long — but the grains are water ice coated in hydrocarbons that fell out of the moon’s orange atmosphere  Space Daily

item.title: Astronauts who have seen the whole Earth at once describe the overview effect — every war and border and argument hanging in silence — and afterwards traffic and small talk can feel strangely far away, like the world seen through glass - Space Daily
item.description: Astronauts who have seen the whole Earth at once describe the overview effect — every war and border and argument hanging in silence — and afterwards traffic and small talk can feel strangely far away, like the world seen through glass  Space Daily

item.title: In 1980, Voyager 1 flew close enough to Titan to reveal its thick nitrogen atmosphere — but the gravity-assist geometry bent the spacecraft permanently out of the planetary plane, trading Uranus and Neptune for one close look at Saturn’s largest moon - Space Daily
